rcomplex 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Comparative co-expression analysis with rcomplex

Reduce orthogroups by merging correlated paralogs

Source: R/orthologs.R
reduce_orthogroups.Rd

Within each ortholog group (HOG), paralogs with Pearson correlation above cor_threshold are merged into a single representative gene via Ward.D2 agglomerative clustering. Merged genes are replaced by their averaged expression profile.

reduce_orthogroups(
  expr_matrix,
  orthologs,
  gene_col = "Species1",
  cor_threshold = 0.7
)

Arguments

expr_matrix

Numeric matrix (genes x samples) with gene identifiers as row names.

orthologs

Data frame with columns Species1 (or the column matching gene row names), Species2, and hog, as returned by parse_orthologs.

gene_col

Character: which column of orthologs contains gene IDs matching row names of expr_matrix (default "Species1").

cor_threshold

Pearson correlation threshold for merging paralogs within a HOG (default 0.7). Higher values are more conservative (fewer merges).

Value

A list with components:

expr_matrix

Reduced expression matrix (genes x samples) with row names. Merged genes have averaged expression.

gene_map

Data frame with columns original and representative, mapping every original gene to its representative in the reduced matrix.

n_original

Number of genes before reduction.

n_reduced

Number of genes after reduction.

n_merged

Number of genes absorbed into representatives.

Details

This is an optional preprocessing step before compute_network. It reduces redundancy from recent duplications where paralogs retain nearly identical expression patterns, shrinking the expression matrix and avoiding combinatorial blowup in downstream clique detection.

Subfunctionalized paralogs (distinct expression programs) are preserved as separate clusters. Zero-variance genes and genes not assigned to any HOG are kept as-is.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
reduced <- reduce_orthogroups(expr_matrix, orthologs)
reduced$expr_matrix  # reduced expression matrix
reduced$gene_map     # original -> representative mapping
} # }

Contents
